prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like assembling a mosaic in a dimly lit room using only your sense of touch—each new word is a cool, smooth tile you fumble to place, trusting that a pattern will emerge even though you can’t yet see the whole picture. At first, your hands grope awkwardly, mismatching shapes and leaving gaps, but with time, your fingers grow sensitive to subtle contours, and the fragments begin to click into place with quiet satisfaction. One day, the lights flicker on, and you step back to find not just a coherent image, but a landscape you helped build—foreign yet familiar, intricate and entirely your own.
